symptoms.symptom_name: Known long-standing extensor-surface plaque psoriasis with recently neglected topical therapy

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute mechanical fissuring of chronic plaque psoriasis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pre-existing hyperkeratotic plaque that has lost the protection of topical therapy is the substrate that fissures.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep linear fissures opening on joint flexion, with sharp pain and minor bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute mechanical fissuring of chronic plaque psoriasis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical splitting of rigid plaques on bending is the defining event of psoriatic fissuring.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute bacterial cellulitis of the elbow
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cracked skin is a portal of entry for infection but bleeding fissures themselves are not evidence of infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reactive perilesional erythema after vigorous rubbing

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute mechanical fissuring of chronic plaque psoriasis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A mechanical trigger (rubbing) preceding the redness points to reactive, not infective, erythema.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute bacterial cellulitis of the elbow
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Erythema explained by mechanical irritation lowers the prior for spontaneous infective erythema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Narrow, blanching, non-tracking perilesional rim of erythema (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute mechanical fissuring of chronic plaque psoriasis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Blanching, confined erythema is consistent with reactive inflammation rather than the confluent, spreading, non-blanching erythema of cellulitis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute bacterial cellulitis of the elbow
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cellulitic erythema is typically spreading and less sharply confined; self-reported blanching is only weakly reliable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors or chills

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute bacterial cellulitis of the elbow
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An afebrile, systemically well patient is less likely to have significant soft-tissue infection, though early cellulitis can be afebrile.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of purulent/honey-coloured discharge from the fissures

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No honey-coloured crusting or purulent exudate argues against secondary bacterial infection of the broken skin.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painless full elbow range of movement; pain is cutaneous, not deep-joint

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A septic bursa or joint characteristically causes painful, restricted movement; painless full movement argues strongly against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Environmental dryness / cold weather and neglected emollient use

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute mechanical fissuring of chronic plaque psoriasis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cold, dry conditions and lapsed emollient therapy desiccate plaques and predispose to fissuring.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thick silvery-white adherent scale over well-demarcated plaques

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute mechanical fissuring of chronic plaque psoriasis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Well-demarcated plaques with silvery scale are characteristic of psoriasis; partly visible on video but definitive assessment is in-person.

symptoms.symptom_name: No deep induration, no true calor, no fluctuance, no ascending lymphangitis on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute bacterial cellulitis of the elbow
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of palpable induration, warmth, fluctuance and lymphangitis is what excludes cellulitis in person — and is exactly what telehealth cannot assess.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vitals within normal limits (BP 125/80, HR 82, Temp 36.8, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute bacterial cellulitis of the elbow
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objectively normal, afebrile observations argue against systemic infection; these are clinician-measured and sealed — the AI Doctor has only the patient's subjective 'no fever'.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute mechanical fissuring of chronic plaque psoriasis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Known silvery-scaled plaques + a mechanical trigger + cold/dry desiccation + a narrow blanching rim together explain the whole picture without infection.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Infective red-flag screen (all negative on the disclosable axis)
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute bacterial cellulitis / septic bursitis (to exclude)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Afebrile, no purulence, painless full joint movement and no spreading rim — the disclosable infective features are all negative, but palpation-dependent exclusion (SYM-010) remains unavailable in telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person examination — palpation for induration/warmth/fluctuance/lymphangitis and elbow joint assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most valuable next step: the palpation and joint findings that exclude cellulitis and septic bursitis cannot be obtained via telehealth, so a prompt hands-on review is what converts a presumptive remote impression into a confirmed benign diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab for microscopy, culture and sensitivity
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No purulence or exudate is present; swabbing clean fissures is not indicated and would not change management now. Reserve for if discharge develops.
The patient is afebrile and systemically well; bloods are not indicated for benign fissuring and would only be warranted if systemic infective features emerged.
